records.ety: [F. venue a coming, arrival, fr. venir to come, L. venire; hence, in English, the place whither the jury are summoned to come. See Come, and cf. Venew, Veney.]

defs: A neighborhood or near place; the place or county in which anything is alleged to have happened; also, the place where an action is laid.
quotes: The twelve men who are to try the cause must be of the same venue where the demand is made.
notes: ☞ In certain cases, the court has power to change the venue, which is to direct the trial to be had in a different county from that where the venue is laid.

defs: A bout; a hit; a turn. See Venew.
